name: Writer’s Evening - Writing Workshop
description:
Every week House of Belonging hosts an hour of uninterrupted writing alone - together!
Whether you are working on a think piece, pouring your heart out in your journal, or drafting the next bestseller, every writer and every medium is welcome here- notebooks, laptops, the arm of your lover, etc. etc.. We'll spend an hour writing quietly and afterwards gather to optionally share and listen to our fellow writer friends. We encourage you to grab a warm bev or a little something to snack on while you write, and stay to mingle with your new pen pals after.
Come soak up the creative energy in the space with us and scribble (or click) the hour away.

name: The Bengal Reader - Book Discussion by Arunava Sinha
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for an evening celebrating The Bengal Reader, the most expansive anthology of Bengali writing in translation published in a single volume. Edited and translated by Arunava Sinha—one of the most prolific translators of our time—this landmark collection opens up two centuries of Bengali literary thought to both Bengali and non-Bengali readers alike.
Arunava Sinha will be in conversation with writer and editor Shinie Antony on 6 March, 5 PM to 7 PM, in a wide-ranging discussion on the making of the anthology, the evolution of Bengali literature, and the politics and possibilities of translation. Together, they will explore how the book traces the intellectual, social, and literary currents of Bengal—from reformist voices like Rammohun Roy and Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay, through Rabindranath Tagore and Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay, to revolutionary and contemporary writers engaging with caste, media, consumerism, and identity.
The Bengal Reader reflects the literary life of Bengal across two centuries—its arguments, revolutions, and reinventions—and brings together voices that have shaped Indian literature and society. This conversation will offer readers an opportunity to engage with the book’s expansive vision and the rich histories it carries, as well as with the craft of translation that makes these works accessible across languages and generations.
Join us for an engaging evening of literary history, translation, and conversation.

name: Oscar Wilde Reader Meetup - A Group discussion for Literature Enthusiasts
description:
"All women become like their mothers. That is their tragedy. No man does, and that is his."
Undeniably one of the funniest men to ever write, Oscar Wilde’s plays, short stories, and novels live on in the Victorian imagination. He was a man who mastered the art of the surface while hiding profound truths beneath it—a rebel in a velvet suit who could dismantle a social hierarchy with a single sentence.
Whether you have long been enthralled by Wilde’s atmospheric prose or are just beginning to step into his intriguing worlds, you are warmly invited to join us this month as we gather to discuss, reflect, and lose ourselves in his unforgettable stories. After all, as Wilde himself famously noted, "The only thing to do with good advice is to pass it on. It is never of any use to oneself."

name: Financial Freedom Starts with Awareness - A Financial Literacy Workshop
description:
Participants will learn the basics of budgeting, investing, and wealth creation, guided through real-world examples and actionable strategies.
“Financial education shouldn’t be intimidating,” said Sathwik N.N., Co-founder of Aatmeeya. “We’re creating a space where anyone, from students to professionals can learn to make their money work better for them.” The event will take place on 07th March, 2026 at Atta Galatta, Indiranagar from 11am to 1pm. Registration is Rs. 299 and open to all interested participants.
For press inquiries contact +91 8296862777.
Age: 18 and above

name: Windows & Mirrors: An Interactive Session by Gayatri  - A session for Adults to read children's books, look out & look within
description:
Should adults read children's books? How can these books spark conversations about diversity, inclusion, and privilege? 'Windows and Mirrors' is a space where adults can reconnect with the joy and curiosity that children's books often evoke. 
During the session, participants will:

Explore Indian children’s literature in print through Rudine Sims Bishop’s framework of “Mirrors, Windows, and Sliding Glass Doors.”
Reflect on their own reading habits and social location using The Indian Wheel of Power and Powerlessness as a lens.
Browse and read a curated selection of children’s books. 
Participate in a facilitated learning circle with guided reflection prompts.

Age: 18 and above
The session blends quiet reading, critical inquiry, and shared dialogue to examine how the stories we consume shape our understanding of identity, belonging, and power.

name: The Tree, The Well & the Drag Queen - Book signing
description:
Join us on a book signing with Salini Vineeth, the author of The Tree, The Well & the Drag Queen, a fantasy novella that follows a queer protagonist’s journey to their ancestral village to confront an uncanny opponent: a wicked jackfruit tree. 
In this book, Salini Vineeth blends folklore, myth and magic with a contemporary exploration of gender identity.  
Centuries ago, a man and a woman in search of an elusive treasure fall into the servitude of a dark force that transmogrifies into a gigantic jackfruit Tree. Generation after generation, their descendants serve the Tree. It’s a vicious cycle of greed and cowardice. 
And then, a voice of dissent. A descendant defies the gender, familial and social expectations. They manage to break free and build an authentic life, far away from the Tree’s malevolent gaze. But then, they realize that the Tree still holds the reins of their destiny. Will the drag queen defeat the Tree and break the vicious cycle of servitude? Will they claim absolute freedom?  
Come, join us on this book signing event and have a chat with the author about drag queens and wicked jackfruit trees.

name: Ode to Broken Things - Book Launch
description:
Join us to celebrate Shruti Kulkarni’s debut poetry collection and reading from her book Ode to Broken Things. The poet will be in conversation with Vinutha Mallya.
Ode to Broken Things is a reflection on what it means to be human, to be a living repository of timestamps of our individual and collective struggles and celebrations and of rebellions and failures. These poems are a quiet observation of our responses to the passing of these events and of our efforts to understand this world that offers experiences of duality — of wars and peacetime, abundance and poverty, of love and hate. Ode to Broken Things is also a prayer and an earnest dream for a peaceful world for everyone, where all hearts are warm and borders are always open.
In Ode to Broken Things, we are taken to the heart of matter — to observe the atomic, the molecular, the granular parts of our inner and outer worlds that we miss amid the unsettling busyness. Ode to Broken Things invites us to feel, see, think and wonder about all that surrounds us. Raw and uncut, these poetic expressions draw us towards hope.
 – Vinutha Mallya [Journalist, editor and lecturer]
Age: 14 and above

name: Boho Art Tote Bag Painting - Workshop
description:
This is a guided Boho Art Tote Bag Painting Workshop, where participants will learn to paint boho-inspired patterns and motifs on a canvas tote bag. The session is designed to be beginner-friendly and relaxing, making it suitable for both first-time painters and art enthusiasts.
This is a 1.5-hour guided workshop, and all required materials will be provided. Participants will take home their hand-painted tote bag at the end of the session.
Age: 10 and above

About the Artist:
Niruba is an artist, doll artist, and art facilitator, and a world record holder in handmade doll making. She is a Fevicryl Certified Professional and Teacher and conducts art and craft workshops across Bangalore for corporates, private events, and cultural spaces. She also runs regular art and craft classes for children from her home studio.

name: Journey of a Song- 150 years of Vande Mataram - An evening of stories,  music and poetry
description:
A poetry. A song. A novel. And their tumultuous journey of 150 years.
Vande Mataram, which was drawn into the vortex of history. Vande Mataram, which moulded history in its own term.
Stories and anecdotes which have stalked the song. Personalities who have drifted into that journey. Facts, myths and misgivings – all of which have painted the canvas and lent to the enigma of a call: Vande Mataram.
Nivedita and Ashis take the audience through this fascinating journey, and sometimes straddle the fault line of fact and fiction.
-x-
Nivedita: a Hindustani Classical vocalist with the World Music album, Autumn Ballet, from Saregama India (erstwhile HMV). A performer and teacher, Nivedita had led a one-hour TV program dedicated to Major Sandeep Unnikrishnan, martyr of Mumbai 26/11, where she sang Vande Mataram in the oldest known tune.
Ashis: a software entrepreneur, a raconteur who has anchored major musical shows, a writer in mainstream newspapers and inflight magazines on travel, music and culture, and author of the book – Gandhi and the London Cabby. From Rumi to Bankim Chandra, Ashis has written about in major newspapers.
Nivedita and Ashis had created a vote song, “Chalo Vote de” which was adopted by the Election Commission of India. Their song was later expanded in ten Indian languages by Janagraha.
Age: 10 and above

name: Atta Galatta presents “Support Group” - Support Group by Nimesh George and Gokul R.
description:
The scars left behind by the terrible experience of sexual abuse during childhood is among the most difficult to heal, which impacts everything from how one perceives oneself, perception of relationships and other people in general. This special edition of support group aims at facilitating a discussion around the challenges of surviving sexual abuse. The gentle discussion will be centered around:
How the abuse has impacted one’s sense of self
How relationship with parents have been impacted
Struggles with experiences in the body
Difficulties in romantic relationship
Feeling unsafe and difficulties in establishing trusting relationships
How to escape the cycle of repeated feelings pertaining to the experience 
How to rempower the self 
If you wish to not refer to your experience and simply be a part of the discussion, you too are welcome.
Age: 18 and above

name: That’s What She Said - A Playback Theatre Performance by Threads and Tales
description:
On the occasion of Women’s Day, we ask a simple question:
Why should women be celebrated only on one day?
Women are powerful, complex, and extraordinary every single day—yet too often treated like second-hand citizens the rest of the year. This performance begins right there, challenging the idea of limiting celebration to a date on the calendar.
Instead, we open a space for real stories.
Stories of being a woman.
Stories of loving, learning from, and living alongside women.
Stories of mothers, teachers, mothers-in-law, partners, sisters, daughters, friends, bosses, colleagues, daughters-in-law—and everything in between.
Stories of how men see the women in their lives.
Stories of embracing the feminine within.
What to Expect
This unique Playback Theatre experience invites the audience to share personal moments and lived experiences. In real time, our performers will transform these stories into spontaneous theatre using movement, music, and dialogue—honest, unscripted, and deeply human.
Threads and Tales is an Applied theater initiative by Lahe Lahe Caravan 
 
⏱️ When: 8th March | 5:30 – 7:00 PM
📍 Where: Atta Galata, Indiranagar
🎟 Tickets: 300 rs per person
Age: 18 and above

name: What They Don’t Tell You About Marriage - Book Launch
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for the launch of What They Don’t Tell You About Marriage, a refreshingly honest and practical guide to long-term relationships. Couples therapist Yashodhara Lal will be in conversation with Radhika Mohta, matchmaker and relationship coach, for a candid discussion on love, conflict, commitment, and everything people usually only Google at 2 a.m.
Drawing on clinical insights and her own two decades of marriage, Yashodhara Lal unpacks the realities of partnership—misunderstandings, unmet expectations, intimacy, money, in-laws, parenting, and more—offering tools to navigate them with clarity and compassion. Expect an open, real talk on what it actually takes to build and sustain a relationship, beyond the fairy-tale version we’re sold.
Age: 18 and above

name: Comics & Animation Workshop - A creative session
description:
If you ever said, “I can’t even draw a straight line,” then you are perfect for comics and cartoons. It is, technically, the art of bad drawing. This June, take doodling in the margins a step ahead—with Bakarmax’s Sumit Kumar as your guide!
Our famous Comics 101 workshop returns on 29th June 2025. Start with no skills and end with a one-page comic written and drawn by you. Book your ticket now, and don’t forget to bring your friends, family, and anyone else (it's all ages) whose wallet you can convince — because the only thing we love more than comics… is your money.
Let’s draw, laugh, and make something ridiculous together. See you there!
Age: 12 and above

name: Between the Pages: A Crochet Bookmark Workshop - A Workshop by Lakshitha
description:
Spend your Saturday evening slowly creating something special with your hands.
In this beginner-friendly workshop, we’ll learn how to crochet a handmade bookmark — a small, meaningful piece you can tuck between the pages of your favourite book or gift to someone who loves to read.
This session is designed by Slip Stitch for complete beginners as well as anyone who simply wants to sit, unwind, and make something beautiful in a warm, book-filled space.
You’ll learn:
• Basic crochet techniques
• How to read a simple pattern
• How to finish and neatly assemble your bookmark
All materials will be provided — just bring yourself (and maybe your current read).
Where: Atta Galatta
When: 14th March
Time: 4:00 PM – 6:00 PM
Price: ₹1100
Age: 20-50
An evening of yarn, stories, and slow creativity.

name: Heartbreak Unfiltered - Book Discussion
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for the launch of Heartbreak Unfiltered: Things Nobody Told You About Love, Loss and Letting Go by Milan Vohra, India’s bestselling romance author, who now turns her lens to the other side of love — heartbreak.
Raw, honest and deeply relatable, Heartbreak Unfiltered brings together 20 real stories from across the world that explore the many faces of heartbreak — betrayal, ghosting, gaslighting, fading love and the slow unravelling of once-beautiful relationships. With her signature wit, warmth and grounded wisdom, Milan goes beyond platitudes and sugar-coating to offer readers something far more valuable: clarity.
Blending storytelling, psychology and practical self-reflection, the book unpacks attachment styles, red flags, love languages and the science behind why we fall so hard — and hurt so deeply. Alongside these moving accounts, readers will find “Ex-ercises” — thoughtful prompts and tools designed to help transform pain into growth, strength and emotional freedom.
At the launch, Milan will be in conversation with Shinie Antony for an intimate and candid discussion on love, loss and letting go in today’s world. The evening will include a reading from the book, followed by their conversation and an audience Q&A.
If you’ve ever loved, lost, questioned yourself, or tried to make sense of a goodbye that didn’t come with closure, this book — and this conversation — is for you.
Because heartbreak isn’t the end. It’s the beginning of becoming whole.

name: Come Sit With Us - A Meetup by My Divorce Guide
description:
It is an intimate, offline gathering designed for those navigating separation, relationship transitions, or quiet personal reinvention. It is a gentle, held space where stories will be shared across the room — without performance, without spotlight, without the pressure to “have it all figured out.”
Some stories will be read aloud. Some will simply be witnessed.
You are welcome to speak, or just sit and listen.
Why this room?
Because separation can feel isolating.
Because being heard can be healing.
Because stories soften shame.
Because sometimes, you just need a space where you can exhale.
Whether you are going through it, supporting someone who is, or reflecting on your own journey — this evening is an invitation to pause, connect, and remember that you are not alone.
Date: March 15, 2026
Location: Atta Galatta, Bangalore
Duration: 2 hours

name: A STORY WALK through INDIRANAGAR - History Walk
description:
On a Saturday morning , Walk through the lanes of INDIRA NAGAR with Vikram Sridhar discovering the lanes through the Folklore , Heritage and Ecology of the area . The walk will begin and culminate at ATTA GALATTA . The tales will revolve around historical , mythical and personal narratives weaved as we discover the area through the walk .
 Date : 21st March ( Saturday)   
 Time : 7 AM
 Starting Point : Atta Galatta
Registration : 349/ per person 
Ticket:https://in.bookmyshow.com/plays/a-story-walk-through-indiranagar-bangalore/ET00387939 
(or) 
Details and GPAY : 9945799224

name: A STORY WALK through DOMLUR  - Story Walk by Vikram Sridhar
description:
On a Sunday morning , Walk through the lanes of DOMLUR , from lores dating back in time from the Madras Presidency , Mysore State to the current IT , Banking and Retail sectors with Vikram Sridhar discovering the lanes through the Folklore , Heritage and Ecology of the rich area which is identified as a village. The walk will begin from Bhandutvas , a theatre collective , and culminate at the Bangalore International Centre . The tales will revolve around historical , mythical and personal narratives weaved as we discover the area through the walk .
The Story walk is supported by Atta Galatta
 *Date* : 22nd March  (Sunday)  2026
 *Time* : 7 AM
 *Starting Point* : Bhandutvas , Domlur
Registration : 349/ per person 
Ticket Link : https://in.bookmyshow.com/plays/a-story-walk-through-domlur-bangalore/ET00465609
(or) 
Details and GPAY : 9945799224

name: My Home is Dissent - Book Launch & Poetry Workshop
description:
My Home is Dissent is a luminous poetry collection by Pooja Ugrani that constructs verse as one might build a home—brick by tender brick, with the mortar of memory, desire, and defiance. Published by Poetrywala, an imprint of Paperwall Publishing, this collection moves fluidly between the everyday and the elemental, where the domestic becomes a site of quiet resistance and reimagining. Ugrani's dissent hums through acts of nurture and refusal, through the courage to remain soft in a world that demands armour. Her poems measure the dimensions of love, labour, and loss through the delicate instruments of memory, architecture, and motherhood. Formally supple and sensorial, cerebral and sensuous, her work folds the architectural and the emotional into one continuum. This world of words writes of labour, love, and dissent as acts of design—as deliberate gestures that hold the self together. Praised by acclaimed authors including Sampurna Chattarji, Maithreyi Karnoor, and poet Srividya Sivakumar, this collection participates physically in the making of poetry, not only through language but also through the palpability of the body's experiences. Here is a poet testing her wings, where dissension may well be a heartbeat away from dissection. It is currently out on Amazon for sale: https://amzn.in/d/0gY3DXTf
 
About the Poet: Pooja Ugrani, architect, teacher and mother, lives her life in two cities, Bombay and Bangalore.
Her poems are included in the anthologies The Shape of a Poem - The Red River Book of Contemporary Erotic Poetry, The Kali project anthology - Invoking the Goddess within/ Indian Women's Voices by Indie Blue Publishing LLC, the Yearbook of Indian Poetry 2022 and Po'try, an anthology of Bangalore poets. Her poem, Gundalamma Palem was shortlisted as one of the forty poems out of over thousand international entries for the Alpine Fellowship 2023. Her poetry has been published in The Punch Magazine, Cafe Dissensus Everyday, Mom Egg Review (New York), Hākārā, Voice and Verse Poetry Magazine (Hong Kong), The Tiger Moth Review (Singapore), The Alipore Post, The Woman Inc - TWI Poetry, and the RIC Journal (Jaipur-Paris). Ugrani was invited to read at the Bangalore Poetry Festival and by the Champaca bookstore at the Bangalore International Center on the occasion of International Womens' Day. She was a featured poet at Tuesdays with the Bard at Urban Solace, Ulsoor, in December 2019. While she is relaxing, Pooja Ugrani explores origami with children and adults, sings Bollywood songs and dreams of a trek in the Himalayas.
Age: 15 and above
